title: Agent Descriptor (v1)
description: Vendor-neutral agent descriptor covering (a) connection: transport + endpoint + auth + acp_capable, (b) config surface: knob list with names, types, defaults, required, secret flag. Conforms to the ncz-os agent-descriptor ADR (docs/adr/0001). Static only - no runtime discovery.

id.description: Stable identifier URI for this descriptor. Format: <vendor-namespace>:agent-descriptor:<implementation-slug>:v<schema-version>

agent_id.description: Stable reverse-DNS identifier for the agent itself (must match across descriptor revisions for the same implementation).

version.description: Implementation version of the agent that emitted this descriptor (free-form semver or date). NOT the schema version.

schema_version.description: Version of the agent-descriptor schema this artifact conforms to. MUST match the major version embedded in `id`.

conformance_level.description: MIF-style conformance level: 1 = identity + connection only; 2 = identity + connection + config surface (knobs).

config_surface.description: Required when conformance_level >= 2; optional otherwise. Describes the agent's user-facing configuration surface (knobs, defaults, secret flags).

acp_capable.description: Whether this agent speaks the Agent Client Protocol (editor <-> agent). Connection descriptor still applies (ACP is transport-agnostic).

extensions.description: Reserved escape hatch for vendor-specific flags. Keys MUST be reverse-DNS.
Reserved escape hatch for descriptor-level vendor extensions. Keys MUST be reverse-DNS.

allOf.description: conformance_level 2 mandates a config_surface (the ncz-os ADR contract); the `config_surface: null` branch is rejected because a null surface at L2 is not a real config surface.
stdin/stdout transport requires no real auth (no network surface); record as null.

endpoint.description: Tagged union: each transport has a different endpoint shape. Use `kind` to discriminate.

source.description: How the agent loads its configuration file (json / toml / yaml / env).

default_path.description: Conventional default file path for the configuration (relative or absolute). Optional; agents may rely purely on env-var overrides.

schema.description: Inline JSON Schema for the underlying config object. Kept inline so a consumer parses one document; mirrors MIF's `package.json` style.

name.description: Identifier of the knob (e.g. 'default_provider', or 'providers[*].auth.var' to scope to a per-array-element sub-knob). MUST be unique within `config_surface.knobs`.

kind.description: Stable type tag. `enum` and `reference` delegate to `enum_values` / `ref` respectively.

default.description: JSON value of the default if defined. Omitted when default is non-deterministic (e.g. system path).

secret.description: True when the knob's value is a credential or token that must be redacted from logs / reports. UI hint only; format/validation is independent.

enum_values.description: When `kind = "enum"`, the permitted values. Order is significant for client UIs.

ref.description: When `kind = "secret_ref"`, the env-var name OR the credential reference key (e.g. `OPENAI_API_KEY`).
